orchestratorOverride.rationale: The single blocking finding is a false positive: the reviewer asserted that username-step entrance focus should land on the primary button `#username-next`, but this contradicts VAL-NAV-018 in the authoritative validation-contract.md, which explicitly carves out: 'or, on step-username, the #username-input Input — which is the primary interactive element — with Tab landing on #username-next next. The user must be able to press Enter immediately upon a step appearing, without first clicking or Tabbing.' The implementation matches the contract (input is focused on entry; priority Enter binding activates the primary). The test that codifies this behavior therefore correctly reflects VAL-NAV-018. The reviewer read the one-line summary in the feature description instead of the authoritative contract assertion. No fix feature is needed. The three suggestedGuidanceUpdates were acted on: (1) features.json verificationSteps scrubbed of `uv run cc-sentiment setup` smoke commands for M1/M2; (2) tui-worker skill already hardened in commit b3daeef with explicit tty-safety rules; (3) validation-contract.md reconciled with RadioSet-only discovery redesign — see reconcile-contract-datatable task output.

typecheck.command: echo "cc-sentiment has no typecheck baseline (no pyright/mypy config in project); skipping per services.yaml policy"

lint.command: cd client && uv run --with ruff ruff check cc_sentiment/tui tests/test_tui.py

blockingIssues.description: Setup username-step entrance focus targets #username-input instead of the step primary action (#username-next), diverging from the feature contract expectation that entrance focus lands on the primary control.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align feature-level verificationSteps templates with mission tty-safety policy by removing tty-attaching smoke commands like `uv run cc-sentiment setup` and requiring Pilot-only verification for M1/M2 features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both shared-visual-primitives and button-contract-and-keyboard reviews surfaced conflict between feature verification command text and mission/skill restrictions forbidding foreground tty-attaching setup runs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen `tui-worker` skill with an explicit lint/checklist item that rejects any feature plan or verification step containing tty-attaching setup invocations, even if written in features.json.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Repeated shared-state observations showed workers following feature verification text that conflicted with mission safety boundaries.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update mission validation guidance to keep contract assertions synchronized with milestone architecture decisions (e.g., discovery DataTable assertions after the RadioSet-only redesign).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: discovery-single-radioset review flagged stale DataTable-oriented assertions in validation-contract.md while implementation and tests are RadioSet-only.

rejectedObservations.observation: Textual `remove_children()` async timing nuance should be added to shared library docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: Ambiguous scope and low confidence as durable cross-mission guidance without broader corroboration.

rejectedObservations.observation: Copy/wrapping work can regress auto-setup username-status back-nav invariants.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already addressed by later milestone feature `auto-setup-and-username-flow-preserved` (commit c470c1e), so not carried forward as unresolved shared guidance.
